speaker: YORK
text: Now, my good lords of Salisbury and Warwick,

speaker: YORK
text: Our simple supper ended, give me leave,

speaker: YORK
text: In this close walk, to satisfy myself

speaker: YORK
text: In craving your opinion of my title,

speaker: YORK
text: Which is infallible, to England’s crown.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My lord, I long to hear it at full.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Sweet York, begin; and if thy claim be good,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The Nevilles are thy subjects to command.

speaker: YORK
text: Then thus:

speaker: YORK
text: Edward the Third, my lords, had seven sons:

speaker: YORK
text: The first, Edward the Black Prince, Prince of Wales;

speaker: YORK
text: The second, William of Hatfield; and the third,

speaker: YORK
text: Lionel, Duke of Clarence; next to whom

speaker: YORK
text: Was John of Gaunt, the Duke of Lancaster;

speaker: YORK
text: The fifth was Edmund Langley, Duke of York;

speaker: YORK
text: The sixth was Thomas of Woodstock, Duke of Gloucester;

speaker: YORK
text: William of Windsor was the seventh and last.

speaker: YORK
text: Edward the Black Prince died before his father

speaker: YORK
text: And left behind him Richard, his only son,

speaker: YORK
text: Who, after Edward the Third’s death, reigned as king

speaker: YORK
text: Till Henry Bolingbroke, Duke of Lancaster,

speaker: YORK
text: The eldest son and heir of John of Gaunt,

speaker: YORK
text: Crowned by the name of Henry the Fourth,

speaker: YORK
text: Seized on the realm, deposed the rightful king,

speaker: YORK
text: Sent his poor queen to France, from whence she came,

speaker: YORK
text: And him to Pomfret; where, as all you know,

speaker: YORK
text: Harmless Richard was murdered traitorously.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Father, the Duke hath told the truth.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thus got the house of Lancaster the crown.

speaker: YORK
text: Which now they hold by force and not by right;

speaker: YORK
text: For Richard, the first son’s heir, being dead,

speaker: YORK
text: The issue of the next son should have reigned.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But William of Hatfield died without an heir.

speaker: YORK
text: The third son, Duke of Clarence, from whose line

speaker: YORK
text: I claim the crown, had issue, Philippa, a daughter,

speaker: YORK
text: Who married Edmund Mortimer, Earl of March.

speaker: YORK
text: Edmund had issue, Roger, Earl of March;

speaker: YORK
text: Roger had issue: Edmund, Anne, and Eleanor.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: This Edmund, in the reign of Bolingbroke,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: As I have read, laid claim unto the crown

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And, but for Owen Glendower, had been king,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Who kept him in captivity till he died.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But to the rest.

speaker: YORK
text: His eldest sister, Anne,

speaker: YORK
text: My mother, being heir unto the crown,

speaker: YORK
text: Married Richard, Earl of Cambridge, who was son

speaker: YORK
text: To Edmund Langley, Edward the Third’s fifth son.

speaker: YORK
text: By her I claim the kingdom. She was heir

speaker: YORK
text: To Roger, Earl of March, who was the son

speaker: YORK
text: Of Edmund Mortimer, who married Philippa,

speaker: YORK
text: Sole daughter unto Lionel, Duke of Clarence.

speaker: YORK
text: So, if the issue of the elder son

speaker: YORK
text: Succeed before the younger, I am king.

speaker: WARWICK
text: What plain proceedings is more plain than this?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Henry doth claim the crown from John of Gaunt,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The fourth son; York claims it from the third.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Till Lionel’s issue fails, his should not reign.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It fails not yet, but flourishes in thee

speaker: WARWICK
text: And in thy sons, fair slips of such a stock.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Then, father Salisbury, kneel we together,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And in this private plot be we the first

speaker: WARWICK
text: That shall salute our rightful sovereign

speaker: WARWICK
text: With honor of his birthright to the crown.

speaker:
SALISBURY
              ,
               
              WARWICK
text: Long live our sovereign Richard, England’s king!

speaker: YORK
text:
We thank you, lords.
                 
                They
                 
                rise
                .
               But I am not your king

speaker: YORK
text: Till I be crowned, and that my sword be stained

speaker: YORK
text: With heart-blood of the house of Lancaster;

speaker: YORK
text: And that’s not suddenly to be performed,

speaker: YORK
text: But with advice and silent secrecy.

speaker: YORK
text: Do you as I do in these dangerous days:

speaker: YORK
text: Wink at the Duke of Suffolk’s insolence,

speaker: YORK
text: At Beaufort’s pride, at Somerset’s ambition,

speaker: YORK
text: At Buckingham, and all the crew of them,

speaker: YORK
text: Till they have snared the shepherd of the flock,

speaker: YORK
text: That virtuous prince, the good Duke Humphrey.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis that they seek; and they, in seeking that,

speaker: YORK
text: Shall find their deaths, if York can prophesy.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: My lord, break we off. We know your mind at full.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My heart assures me that the Earl of Warwick

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall one day make the Duke of York a king.

speaker: YORK
text: And, Neville, this I do assure myself:

speaker: YORK
text: Richard shall live to make the Earl of Warwick

speaker: YORK
text: The greatest man in England but the King.
